OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "I've been watching a lot of misinformation online lately, it's really getting under my skin. As someone who came from another country to Naples, I can tell you firsthand how important it is to have truthful information when making decisions for your family or even just understanding the world around us here in Italy.

Looking at my personality scores - openness 30 which is low, conscientiousness 9 also quite low - but moderate extraversion and agreeableness. I'm not naturally inclined towards deep analysis of complex digital issues, but on the other hand, when it comes to social matters or helping out friends with their confusion about what's real online, that's something close to my heart.

I think managing fake content starts simply: don't just click every link without thinking. Check multiple sources if you can find them before believing anything too fast. When I clean this floor under my feet at work here in Naples, being thorough is essential; checking for dirt means double-checking facts online.

Another thing that helps me personally - and maybe others like us who are immigrants trying to settle down with our families - is knowing where you stand religiously speaking. My Christian beliefs teach me about truthfulness through Jesus' words. But let's not get too deep into theology if it doesn't appeal directly.

The fact I'm single but have one child keeps things simple though sometimes challenging financially as a cleaner working full-time here in Naples without much formal education beyond lower secondary level; still reading everything carefully for our well-being.",
  "reasoning_summary": "I approached this topic by drawing from my personal experiences as an immigrant living in Naples and the importance of reliable information. I considered how misinformation affects daily decisions, especially those involving family welfare.

Using simple language based on my low openness score suited me better than technical jargon or complex theories that require higher cognitive abilities.

I incorporated elements like checking multiple sources before believing something to emphasize thoroughness - similar to being careful while cleaning a floor.",
  "tone": "Calm, practical and empathetic with some personal reflection",
  "citations": [
    "Personal experience as an immigrant in Naples"
  ],
  "safety_notes": "",
  "used_persona_fields":["age", "gender", "occupation", "city_of_living", "education_level", "psychological_feature", "religious_beliefs"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've been watching a lot of misinformation online lately, it's really getting under my skin. As someone who came from another country to Naples, I can tell you firsthand how important it is to have truthful information when making decisions for your family or even just understanding the world around us here in Italy.

Looking at my personality scores - openness 30 which is low, conscientiousness 9 also quite low - but moderate extraversion and agreeableness. I'm not naturally inclined towards deep analysis of complex digital issues, but on the other hand, when it comes to social matters or helping out friends with their confusion about what's real online, that's something close to my heart.

I think managing fake content starts simply: don't just click every link without thinking. Check multiple sources if you can find them before believing anything too fast. When I clean this floor under my feet at work here in Naples, being thorough is essential; checking for dirt means double-checking facts online.

Another thing that helps me personally - and maybe others like us who are immigrants trying to settle down with our families - is knowing where you stand religiously speaking. My Christian beliefs teach me about truthfulness through Jesus' words. But let's not get too deep into theology if it doesn't appeal directly.

The fact I'm single but have one child keeps things simple though sometimes challenging financially as a cleaner working full-time here in Naples without much formal education beyond lower secondary level; still reading everything carefully for our well-being.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I approached this topic by drawing from my personal experiences as an immigrant living in Naples and the importance of reliable information. I considered how misinformation affects daily decisions, especially those involving family welfare.

Using simple language based on my low openness score suited me better than technical jargon or complex theories that require higher cognitive abilities.

I incorporated elements like checking multiple sources before believing something to emphasize thoroughness - similar to being careful while cleaning a floor.
response_parsed.tone: Calm, practical and empathetic with some personal reflection
citations: Personal experience as an immigrant in Naples
